Front Desk Officer Job at Solapower Limited




        
    

        
        Solapower Limited (Baling Steel) is a Chinese steel manufacturer and supplier based in Yueyang, Hunan Province, China. The company has been in operation since 1998 and has over 25 years of experience in the steel industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Salary
N150,000 - N300,000 / month
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: email:[email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Manager",
  "description": "Goldplates Feast House -&nbsp;We are a dynamic and rapidly growing Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) brand committed to excellence in food quality, customer satisfaction, and operational standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase II, Lagos
Department: Information Technology
Reports To: Management / Head of Operations

Job Summary


	The IT Manager is responsible for overseeing and maintaining all technology infrastructure across the organization, including the Head Office and all restaurant outlets.
	The role ensures smooth operation of Point of Sale (POS) systems, internet connectivity, network infrastructure, digital platforms, and IT equipment required for efficient business operations.
	The IT Manager will also support operational efficiency by implementing reliable technology systems, minimizing downtime, ensuring proper IT asset management, and providing technical support to all business locations.


Key Responsibilities
IT Infrastructure Management:


	Manage and maintain IT infrastructure across Head Office and all restaurant outlets.
	Ensure stable internet connectivity and network performance across all locations.
	Supervise installation, configuration, and maintenance of IT equipment including:
	
		POS systems
		Desktop computers and laptops
		Receipt printers and office printers
		Routers, switches, and networking devices
	
	
	Monitor system performance and resolve technical issues promptly to avoid disruption to operations.


POS &amp; Restaurant Technology Management:


	Ensure all Point of Sale (POS) systems in outlets are functional and updated.
	Troubleshoot POS errors affecting order processing, billing, and reporting.
	Coordinate POS updates, menu uploads, and pricing changes.
	Work closely with Operations and Finance to ensure POS data accuracy and reporting reliability.


Multi-Outlet IT Support:


	Provide IT support across all restaurant outlets and Head Office.
	Respond promptly to technical issues affecting daily operations.
	Develop and manage a system for outlets to report IT issues.
	Schedule periodic technical checks for all outlets to prevent system failures.


Network &amp; System Security:


	Ensure all company systems and networks are secure and protected from unauthorized access.
	Implement and monitor cybersecurity best practices.
	Manage user access permissions for systems and software.
	Ensure proper data backup procedures and recovery plans are in place.


Digital Platform &amp; Online Order Integration:


	Support the integration and functionality of digital platforms including:
	
		Online ordering systems
		Delivery platforms
		Website or digital sales channels
	
	
	Ensure seamless synchronization between online platforms and POS systems.


CCTV &amp; Security Systems Support:


	Ensure CCTV systems across Head Office and outlets are properly installed and functioning.
	Handle technical maintenance and troubleshooting of CCTV equipment.
	Maintain proper system storage capacity for CCTV recordings.
	Provide technical support to the Chief Security Officer (CSO) when footage is required for investigation or review.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain a comprehensive IT asset register for all technology equipment across the organization.
	Track the allocation and movement of IT equipment across outlets.
	Ensure proper documentation for all issued devices including:
	
		POS terminals
		Laptops and desktops
		Tablets
		Routers and networking devices
	
	
	Conduct periodic verification of IT assets to prevent loss or misuse.


IT Audit &amp; Compliance:

Conduct periodic IT audits across outlets to ensure systems are functioning properly.


	Verify that all outlets are using approved software and systems.
	Identify technology risks or system inefficiencies and recommend improvements.
	Ensure compliance with company IT policies and operational procedures.


New Outlet IT Setup:


	Lead the technology setup for new restaurant outlets including:
	
		POS installation
		Internet and network configuration
		System integration
		Hardware setup
	
	
	Ensure all IT systems are operational before the outlet begins operations.


IT Procurement &amp; Vendor Management:


	Recommend and procure appropriate IT equipment and software.
	Manage relationships with IT vendors and service providers.
	Evaluate new technology solutions that can improve operational efficiency.


Staff Support &amp; Training:


	Provide technical support to staff across all departments.
	Train outlet staff on proper usage of POS systems and basic IT tools.
	Develop simple user guides where necessary.


Reporting &amp; Documentation:


	Prepare periodic IT reports for Management covering:
	
		IT system performance
		System failures or incidents
		Asset status
		Technology upgrade recommendations
	
	
	Maintain proper documentation of all IT systems and configurations.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hirehiverecruits@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;The CSO is responsible for monitoring, security surveillance, and investigations, while the IT Manager ensures the CCTV systems are technically functional and operational.",

IT Manager Job at Goldplates Feast House




        
    

        
        Goldplates Feast House - We are a dynamic and rapidly growing Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) brand committed to excellence in food quality, customer satisfaction, and operational standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Manager

Job Location: Lekki Phase II, Lagos
Department: Information Technology
Reports To: Management / Head of Operations

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The IT Manager is responsible for overseeing and maintaining all technology infrastructure across the organization, including the Head Office and all restaurant outlets.
	The role ensures smooth operation of Point of Sale (POS) systems, internet connectivity, network infrastructure, digital platforms, and IT equipment required for efficient business operations.
	The IT Manager will also support operational efficiency by implementing reliable technology systems, minimizing downtime, ensuring proper IT asset management, and providing technical support to all business locations.


Key Responsibilities
IT Infrastructure Management:


	Manage and maintain IT infrastructure across Head Office and all restaurant outlets.
	Ensure stable internet connectivity and network performance across all locations.
	Supervise installation, configuration, and maintenance of IT equipment including:
	
		POS systems
		Desktop computers and laptops
		Receipt printers and office printers
		Routers, switches, and networking devices
	
	
	Monitor system performance and resolve technical issues promptly to avoid disruption to operations.


POS & Restaurant Technology Management:


	Ensure all Point of Sale (POS) systems in outlets are functional and updated.
	Troubleshoot POS errors affecting order processing, billing, and reporting.
	Coordinate POS updates, menu uploads, and pricing changes.
	Work closely with Operations and Finance to ensure POS data accuracy and reporting reliability.


Multi-Outlet IT Support:


	Provide IT support across all restaurant outlets and Head Office.
	Respond promptly to technical issues affecting daily operations.
	Develop and manage a system for outlets to report IT issues.
	Schedule periodic technical checks for all outlets to prevent system failures.


Network & System Security:


	Ensure all company systems and networks are secure and protected from unauthorized access.
	Implement and monitor cybersecurity best practices.
	Manage user access permissions for systems and software.
	Ensure proper data backup procedures and recovery plans are in place.


Digital Platform & Online Order Integration:


	Support the integration and functionality of digital platforms including:
	
		Online ordering systems
		Delivery platforms
		Website or digital sales channels
	
	
	Ensure seamless synchronization between online platforms and POS systems.


CCTV & Security Systems Support:


	Ensure CCTV systems across Head Office and outlets are properly installed and functioning.
	Handle technical maintenance and troubleshooting of CCTV equipment.
	Maintain proper system storage capacity for CCTV recordings.
	Provide technical support to the Chief Security Officer (CSO) when footage is required for investigation or review.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain a comprehensive IT asset register for all technology equipment across the organization.
	Track the allocation and movement of IT equipment across outlets.
	Ensure proper documentation for all issued devices including:
	
		POS terminals
		Laptops and desktops
		Tablets
		Routers and networking devices
	
	
	Conduct periodic verification of IT assets to prevent loss or misuse.


IT Audit & Compliance:

Conduct periodic IT audits across outlets to ensure systems are functioning properly.


	Verify that all outlets are using approved software and systems.
	Identify technology risks or system inefficiencies and recommend improvements.
	Ensure compliance with company IT policies and operational procedures.


New Outlet IT Setup:


	Lead the technology setup for new restaurant outlets including:
	
		POS installation
		Internet and network configuration
		System integration
		Hardware setup
	
	
	Ensure all IT systems are operational before the outlet begins operations.


IT Procurement & Vendor Management:


	Recommend and procure appropriate IT equipment and software.
	Manage relationships with IT vendors and service providers.
	Evaluate new technology solutions that can improve operational efficiency.


Staff Support & Training:


	Provide technical support to staff across all departments.
	Train outlet staff on proper usage of POS systems and basic IT tools.
	Develop simple user guides where necessary.


Reporting & Documentation:


	Prepare periodic IT reports for Management covering:
	
		IT system performance
		System failures or incidents
		Asset status
		Technology upgrade recommendations
	
	
	Maintain proper documentation of all IT systems and configurations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: The CSO is responsible for monitoring, security surveillance, and investigations, while the IT Manager ensures the CCTV systems are technically functional and operational.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "GTI Microfinance Limited was incorporated in August 2007 as a limited liability company and started its operations in 2008 at No. 127 Obafemi Awolowo Road, Ikeja.

In October 2013, it was licensed as a State Wide Microfinance Bank and moved its Head Office to No. 4 Tinubu Street, Central Business District, Marina Lagos. Other branches are located at Mushin, Abattoir in Agege, Surulere and Arena.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Accountant is responsible for reconciliation and settlement activities and will provide revenue assurance to the bank.


Duties and Responsibilities


	Ensure daily reconciliation and posting of all of all transactions and provide daily/weekly reconciliation and settlement reports.
	Support the E-business team in resolving settlement and reconciliation matters.
	Monitor income/fees and ensure value is promptly transferred to the bank.
	Verify the incoming and outgoing payments on e-channel accounts.
	Prepare daily and weekly report on over/under-settlement of transactions.
	Liaising regularly with various bank account officers on issues relating to direct debits, standing orders, unidentified credits and charges from time to time.
	Monitors the deduction on loan and facilities with various banks and reconciles with terms and conditions in of the offer.
	Develop and maintain business relations with payment partners.
	Liaise with the settlement bank and payment processing companies on potential under- settlement and ensure the resolution of issues identified in line with the company&rsquo;s policies and procedures.
	Rendition of monthly returns and processing of associated payments for FIRS, LIRS and PFAs within seven days of deduction.
	Sending daily dashboard report to management.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field; MBA or professional accounting qualification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA, CPA) is highly preferred.
	3 - 5 years of progressive experience in finance
	Proven track record of operational excellence in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment.
	In-depth knowledge of financial regulations, risk management, and compliance standards in Nigeria.
	Strong analytical, decision-making, and communication skills.
	Experience in leading high-performing teams and driving organizational change.
	Proficiency in financial software; advanced Excel skills required.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;career@gti.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at GTI Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        GTI Microfinance Limited was incorporated in August 2007 as a limited liability company and started its operations in 2008 at No. 127 Obafemi Awolowo Road, Ikeja.

In October 2013, it was licensed as a State Wide Microfinance Bank and moved its Head Office to No. 4 Tinubu Street, Central Business District, Marina Lagos. Other branches are located at Mushin, Abattoir in Agege, Surulere and Arena.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Accountant is responsible for reconciliation and settlement activities and will provide revenue assurance to the bank.


Duties and Responsibilities


	Ensure daily reconciliation and posting of all of all transactions and provide daily/weekly reconciliation and settlement reports.
	Support the E-business team in resolving settlement and reconciliation matters.
	Monitor income/fees and ensure value is promptly transferred to the bank.
	Verify the incoming and outgoing payments on e-channel accounts.
	Prepare daily and weekly report on over/under-settlement of transactions.
	Liaising regularly with various bank account officers on issues relating to direct debits, standing orders, unidentified credits and charges from time to time.
	Monitors the deduction on loan and facilities with various banks and reconciles with terms and conditions in of the offer.
	Develop and maintain business relations with payment partners.
	Liaise with the settlement bank and payment processing companies on potential under- settlement and ensure the resolution of issues identified in line with the company’s policies and procedures.
	Rendition of monthly returns and processing of associated payments for FIRS, LIRS and PFAs within seven days of deduction.
	Sending daily dashboard report to management.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field; MBA or professional accounting qualification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA, CPA) is highly preferred.
	3 - 5 years of progressive experience in finance
	Proven track record of operational excellence in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment.
	In-depth knowledge of financial regulations, risk management, and compliance standards in Nigeria.
	Strong analytical, decision-making, and communication skills.
	Experience in leading high-performing teams and driving organizational change.
	Proficiency in financial software; advanced Excel skills required.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Engineer",
  "description": "O.K. Isokariari Nigeria Limited is a leading Engineering, Procurement and Construction company with over 50 years of work experience. We are known for our pragmatic value delivery, which flows from our people centric approach, committed professionals and prudent management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Project Engineer will provide technical support to the Project Manager. Successful candidate will be responsible for coordinating all technical activities, including submittals, RFI (Request for Information) management, and ensuring that the work performed on-site aligns perfectly with the approved blueprints and specifications.


Responsibilities


	Technical Coordination: Review and process shop drawings and submittals. Identify and resolve technical discrepancies between architectural, structural, and MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing) drawings.
	Documentation Management: Manage the RFI process, ensuring timely responses from consultants to prevent site delays. Maintain &quot;As-Built&quot; drawings and project O&amp;M (Operations &amp; Maintenance) manuals.
	Schedule &amp; Progress Tracking: Assist in developing and updating the Project Master Schedule. Track material deliveries and subcontractor progress to ensure milestones are met.
	Quality &amp; Safety Compliance: Conduct site inspections to verify that the installation matches the design intent and meets local building codes. Ensure all subcontractors adhere to the company&#39;s safety manual.
	Financial Assistance: Assist in the preparation of monthly progress valuations and variations. Help track project costs against the established budget.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering, or a related field.
	7 - 10 years of experience in on-site construction engineering or project coordination.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Primavera P6 or MS Project, and Procore (or similar Project Management software).
	Strong problem-solving abilities, excellent written communication, and the ability to read complex blueprints.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@okisokariari.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Project Engineer Job at O.K. Isokariari Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        O.K. Isokariari Nigeria Limited is a leading Engineering, Procurement and Construction company with over 50 years of work experience. We are known for our pragmatic value delivery, which flows from our people centric approach, committed professionals and prudent management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Engineer
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Project Engineer will provide technical support to the Project Manager. Successful candidate will be responsible for coordinating all technical activities, including submittals, RFI (Request for Information) management, and ensuring that the work performed on-site aligns perfectly with the approved blueprints and specifications.


Responsibilities


	Technical Coordination: Review and process shop drawings and submittals. Identify and resolve technical discrepancies between architectural, structural, and MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing) drawings.
	Documentation Management: Manage the RFI process, ensuring timely responses from consultants to prevent site delays. Maintain "As-Built" drawings and project O&M (Operations & Maintenance) manuals.
	Schedule & Progress Tracking: Assist in developing and updating the Project Master Schedule. Track material deliveries and subcontractor progress to ensure milestones are met.
	Quality & Safety Compliance: Conduct site inspections to verify that the installation matches the design intent and meets local building codes. Ensure all subcontractors adhere to the company's safety manual.
	Financial Assistance: Assist in the preparation of monthly progress valuations and variations. Help track project costs against the established budget.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, or a related field.
	7 - 10 years of experience in on-site construction engineering or project coordination.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Primavera P6 or MS Project, and Procore (or similar Project Management software).
	Strong problem-solving abilities, excellent written communication, and the ability to read complex blueprints.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Maintenance Executive (Mechanical)&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Mowe / Ibafo - Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	An Agro-Processing Plant is seeking a hands-on and experienced Maintenance Executive (Mechanical) to support the efficient operation of their soya processing plant.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring the reliability, maintenance, and continuous operation of plant machinery and equipment.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform preventive and corrective maintenance on mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment within the plant.
	Conduct routine inspectionsand promptly repair faulty machinery to minimize production downtime.
	Assist with the installation, testing, and commissioningof new machines and equipment.
	Diagnose and resolve mechanical and process-related issueson production lines.
	Maintain accurate maintenance logs and equipment performance records.
	Ensure all maintenance activities comply with plant safety standards and SOPs.
	Conduct machine safety inspectionsand guide operators on proper machine handling.
	Identify opportunities to improve equipment efficiency, reliability, and lifespan.
	Supervise junior technicians and coordinate with contractors when necessary.
	Prepare weekly and monthly maintenance reportsfor the Head of Department.
	Participate in maintenance meetings and contribute to operational improvement initiatives.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Minimum qualification:&nbsp;OND in Mechanical Engineering or related field (HND/BSc will be an added advantage).
	Experience:&nbsp;3&ndash;5 years of hands-on maintenance experience in an agro-industrial plant, preferably soya processing, oil milling, or grain processing.


Technical Skills:
Candidates must have practical experience working with the following equipment:


	Pumps, conveyors, and motors
	Expellers and extruders
	Crackers and hammer mills
	Destoners, drum sieves, and separators
	Preventive maintenance systems and mechanical troubleshooting.


Competencies:


	Strong hands-on technical abilityand mechanical aptitude
	Ability to work independently and within a team
	Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills
	High attention to detail with focus on reducing machine downtime
	Good communication and reporting skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Maintenance Executive (Mechanical)  Job at Konvert Talent Global Concept




        
    

        
        Konvert Talent Global Concept is a full-fledged Human Resource Solutions Provider, incorporated in 2021 with its Head Office in Ogun State, Nigeria. We specialize in delivering practical, reliable, and scalable HR solutions that support organizational growth and operational efficiency.

At Konvert Talent Global Concept, we pride ourselves on professionalism, integrity, and responsiveness. We serve as a trusted HR partner to organizations across various sectors, helping them optimize workforce performance while remaining compliant with regulatory and best-practice standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Executive (Mechanical)

Job Location: Mowe / Ibafo - Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	An Agro-Processing Plant is seeking a hands-on and experienced Maintenance Executive (Mechanical) to support the efficient operation of their soya processing plant.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring the reliability, maintenance, and continuous operation of plant machinery and equipment.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform preventive and corrective maintenance on mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment within the plant.
	Conduct routine inspectionsand promptly repair faulty machinery to minimize production downtime.
	Assist with the installation, testing, and commissioningof new machines and equipment.
	Diagnose and resolve mechanical and process-related issueson production lines.
	Maintain accurate maintenance logs and equipment performance records.
	Ensure all maintenance activities comply with plant safety standards and SOPs.
	Conduct machine safety inspectionsand guide operators on proper machine handling.
	Identify opportunities to improve equipment efficiency, reliability, and lifespan.
	Supervise junior technicians and coordinate with contractors when necessary.
	Prepare weekly and monthly maintenance reportsfor the Head of Department.
	Participate in maintenance meetings and contribute to operational improvement initiatives.


Qualifications & Experience


	Minimum qualification: OND in Mechanical Engineering or related field (HND/BSc will be an added advantage).
	Experience: 3–5 years of hands-on maintenance experience in an agro-industrial plant, preferably soya processing, oil milling, or grain processing.


Technical Skills:
Candidates must have practical experience working with the following equipment:


	Pumps, conveyors, and motors
	Expellers and extruders
	Crackers and hammer mills
	Destoners, drum sieves, and separators
	Preventive maintenance systems and mechanical troubleshooting.


Competencies:


	Strong hands-on technical abilityand mechanical aptitude
	Ability to work independently and within a team
	Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills
	High attention to detail with focus on reducing machine downtime
	Good communication and reporting skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
